executive_summary: Build workflow dispatch failed due to missing or inaccessible GitHub Actions workflow file. The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml was not found in the repository, resulting in HTTP 404 errors across all build attempts. This is a critical configuration issue that prevents any build execution.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found error. The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository or is not accessible with the current permissions.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml is missing from the repository at loginkarun/scib_demo on branch main. This could be due to: (1) The workflow file was never created, (2) The workflow file was deleted, (3) The workflow file exists but the GitHub token lacks workflow/actions permissions, or (4) GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, testing, and deployment processes.

errors.title: Workflow Dispatch API Authentication/Permission Issue
errors.description: The GitHub API token may lack the necessary workflow or actions permissions to dispatch workflows, or GitHub Actions may be disabled for the repository.
errors.error_message: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled
errors.root_cause: The GitHub personal access token (PAT) used for API calls may not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow dispatches. Alternatively, GitHub Actions may be disabled in the repository settings.
errors.impact: Even if the workflow file exists, builds cannot be triggered programmatically via API. This affects automated build triggers and CI/CD pipeline orchestration.

errors.title: Build Workflow Tool Unavailability
errors.description: The BuildWorkflowTool was not available in the current tool set during build attempts 2 and 3, indicating a potential tool configuration or availability issue.
errors.error_message: BuildWorkflowTool not available in current tool set
errors.root_cause: The build orchestration system attempted to use a BuildWorkflowTool that was not registered or available in the tool set. This suggests a configuration mismatch or missing tool registration.
errors.impact: Fallback build mechanisms failed, compounding the primary workflow file issue. This reduces resilience and error recovery capabilities.

recommended_fixes.title: Create GitHub Actions Workflow File
recommended_fixes.description: Create the missing .github/workflows/build.yml file in the repository with proper Maven build configuration.
recommended_fixes.automated_action: Use GitHub Feature Branch Script Committer to create .github/workflows/build.yml with standard Maven build workflow including checkout, JDK setup, Maven build, and test execution steps.
manual_steps: 1. Create .github/workflows directory if it doesn't exist
2. Create build.yml file with workflow_dispatch trigger
3. Configure Maven build steps with proper JDK version
4. Add test execution and artifact publishing steps
5. Commit and push to main branch
recommended_fixes.validation: Verify workflow file exists by checking repository via GitHub UI or API. Test workflow dispatch via API to confirm 200 response.

recommended_fixes.title: Verify GitHub Token Permissions
recommended_fixes.description: Ensure the GitHub personal access token has the 'workflow' scope enabled to allow workflow dispatch operations.
recommended_fixes.automated_action: Not automatable - requires manual token regeneration with proper scopes.
manual_steps: 1. Go to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Generate new token or edit existing token
recommended_fixes.validation: Test API call to workflow dispatch endpoint - should return 204 No Content on success instead of 404.

recommended_fixes.title: Enable GitHub Actions for Repository
recommended_fixes.description: Verify that GitHub Actions is enabled in the repository settings.
recommended_fixes.automated_action: Not automatable - requires repository admin access.
manual_steps: 1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Ensure 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows' is selected
3. Verify Actions permissions are not restricted
4. Check workflow permissions allow read and write
recommended_fixes.validation: Confirm Actions tab is visible in repository and workflows can be viewed/triggered.

compliance_notes.audit_trail: All build attempts logged with timestamps and error details. No successful builds executed due to missing workflow configuration.
compliance_notes.security_considerations: GitHub token permissions should follow principle of least privilege. Workflow scope should only be granted to tokens that require workflow dispatch capabilities.
compliance_notes.remediation_tracking: Track workflow file creation and token permission updates through version control and access logs.

analyzer_notes: No GitHub Actions run ID available due to workflow dispatch failure. Analysis based on HTTP 404 error responses from workflow dispatch API endpoint. This is a pre-build configuration failure rather than a Maven build failure.
